title: Add Trust Layer capability and intent guards
outOfScope: Human Decision Request persistence and decision recording

sources.reason: Defines Task 2 capability, ambiguous-intent, constraint-conflict, and Success Criteria guard scope and acceptance.
Provides the Task 1 schema contract for project-owned capability declarations.
Provides the Task 1 schema contract for Work Item Success Criteria declarations.
Declares repository-owned language, build, boundary, and critical-path capabilities.
Defines Intent, Preflight Review, Evidence over Self-Declaration, and Work Item boundaries.

riskAssessment.reason: The change adds deterministic preflight signals and project-owned declarations without executing project operations.

executionDecision.reason: Task 2 scope, evidence, and deterministic verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review that project-owned capability declarations are authoritative, ambiguous intent fails closed, and no guard infers unsupported capability from free text.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Project-owned capability and Success Criteria declarations validate against the Task 1 schemas and are consumed by the guards.
Supported capability requests reach ready while unsupported capability, ambiguous intent, and conflicting constraints produce deterministic non-ready evidence.
Preflight includes capability, intent, constraint, and Success Criteria signals without inferring missing evidence.
Positive, negative, malformed-declaration, and zero-change-before-confirmation tests pass.
guidelines: Project-owned declarations remain the source of truth for capability matching.
Guard output must be deterministic and machine-readable.

intent.problem: Preflight currently evaluates generic Contract completeness but cannot prove that a requested change fits the repository capability boundary or that intent and success criteria are sufficiently specific.
constraints: Use project-owned declarations and the Task 1 schemas as evidence.
Fail closed on unsupported capability, ambiguous intent, conflicting constraints, or incomplete Success Criteria.
Keep the implementation standard-library based and independent of any specific LLM.
intent.rationale: Deterministic preflight signals make implementation readiness auditable before code changes begin and provide the evidence needed for later human-decision protocol work.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the sequential Trust Layer execution plan, including governance-path changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A request matching a declared repository capability and complete Success Criteria reaches ready
Unsupported capability, ambiguous intent, conflicting constraints, or incomplete criteria remains non-ready with evidence
